blocks.text: Southern Hills Hospital and Medical Center (COCSNV)
* ALL edits or amendments must be made on the electronic/computer
History of balance issues with multiple falls
Patient is a 78-year-old female patient with a past medical history of vertigo, chronic balance
issues due to enlarged ventricles that she uses a cane/walker/wheelchair to get around most
days, multiple falls and incontinence who presents to the emergency department today after
a fall that happened shortly prior to arrival. She presents emergency department today via
EMS. Patient reports she has had more than 12 falls in the last several months. Patient
reports that today she was walking around using her walker outside in her garden and fell
landing on her left hip. She reports she did not hit her head. Patient denied dizziness,
palpitations or chest pain prior to fall. No LOC or sudden weakness. she reports she had a
glass of white wine when she was walking outside. Does not report any chest pain
abdominal pain constipation or diarrhea. Patient reports she had her similar chronic balance
issues that cause her to fall. She has been dealing with the balance issues for last several
History of balance issues with multiple falls
NPO at midnight, pending left hip surgery tomorrow
Oxygen as needed to maintain saturations above 92%
Reconcile home medications and continue: Patient does not take any home meds
Patient seen and evaluated at bedside. NPO. Plan for left hip cephalomedullary nail
scheduled today at 2:00 p.m.. Pain control PRNs on. Patient most likely will
postoperatively need placement in rehab versus SNF.
Pt seen and evaluated at bedside. Pt Left hip cephulmedullary nail surgery completed. Pt
pain is controlled. Pt recieved dose of vancomycin post-op. Plan for aspirin 81mg BID for 6
weeks, Follouw up in 2 weeks. On day of discharge, patient had stable labs and vitals. Pt
was cleared by orthopadic surgery. Pt sent out on home meds and aspirin 81mg BID for 6
weeks. Pt disposition to home w/ home PT, patient in agreement. Pt to f/u with Ortho Surg in
2 weeks, and PCP. Pt Pt was encouraged to see Neurology inpatient due to her hx of
enlarged ventricles and gait dysfunction, but patient refused, but said she would consider
following up in the outpt setting. Pt sent with referral and instructed to follow up with Desert
Neurology. Answered any questinos or concerns appropriately.
Start taking the following new medications:
ENT: moist mucosal membranes, normal ear left
Neck: full range of motion, non-tender
Cardiovascular: normal heart sounds, regular rate & rhythm
Respiratory: aerating well, clear to auscultation
Extremities: moves all, normal capillary refill
Musculoskeletal: Left lower extremity is shortened and slightly externally rotated. Limited
Neuro/CNS: alert, oriented X 3, normal speech
Psychiatry: normal affect, normal judgment/insight, normal mood
Discharge to: Home Health wPlan of Care
Additional Discharge Routines: PCP Follow-Up, Consultant Follow-Up
PCP follow up timeframe: In 4 days
PLEASE CALL FOR APPOINTMENT. Repeat CBC.
Consult follow up timeframe: In 1-2 weeks
Electronically Signed by GHASHAM, HUSSEIN DO R1 on 04/01/26 at 1153
